Artifact PassportVault · scope · rollback · reuse

Artifact Vault · cross-institutional reuse

Reusable capability
must carry proof,
not just promise.

Run a local ceremony that turns a candidate workflow, tool, policy, router, eval, or capability package into a proof-carrying artifact passport. Scope, provenance, evidence, rollback, and public/private boundaries decide whether reuse is allowed.

Issue a passportOpen Upgrade Foundry

No user data wanted. This page is a local public demonstration. No account. No form. No cookies. No analytics. No storage. No wallet. No network request. No token route. Do not enter personal, confidential, regulated, customer, wallet, seed phrase, private-key, or proprietary information.

ARTIFACT PASSPORTWAITING
Ψartifact
versionproofevalscoperollbackreuse
L0conformance
0%reuse readiness
0external actions
01

Define the candidate artifact

02

Adoption passport

Artifact ID—
Version hash—
Selection statusWAITING
Reuse right—
WAITING_FOR_RUN

Issue a passport to see whether the artifact may be reused, held for review, canaried, sandboxed, or rejected.

Passport score—
Conformance—
Scope—
Private leakage risk—
03

Passport gates

A high score cannot bypass a missing rollback target, weak public/private boundary, absent provenance, incomplete proof history, or missing challenge window.

04

Proof-Carrying Artifact JSON

Issue a passport to generate a ProofCarryingArtifactPassport.
01Immutable identity

Reusable capability begins with a stable artifact id and version hash.

02Evidence history

ProofPackets, evals, costs, risks, and replay paths explain why reuse is allowed.

03Scope and rollback

Adoption rights are scoped, canaried, challengeable, and rollback-ready.

04Private intelligence stays private

The passport carries public proof commitments, not confidential traces.

© MontrealAI · Proof-Carrying Artifact Passport · browser-local public demonstration · no user data wanted · no wallet · no network · no transaction broadcast · Privacy · Terms · Legal · AGIALPHA Boundary
